title: SelectionSets Object
description: The SelectionSets object is used to create and access existing selection sets. In the user interface, selection sets are created by selecting geometry and then running the "Create Selection Set" command from the context menu. All existing selection sets are shown in a "Selection Sets" folder in the browser.

methods.name: add
methods.description: Adds a new SelectionSet to the parent product.

methods.name: classType
methods.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().

methods.name: item
methods.description: Returns the specified SelectionSet object using an index into the collection.

methods.name: itemByName
methods.description: Returns the specified SelectionSet object using the name of the selection set.

properties.name: count
properties.description: Returns the number of SelectionSet objects in the collection.

properties.name: isValid
properties.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

properties.name: objectType
properties.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object.   It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

add.title: SelectionSets.add Method
add.description: Adds a new SelectionSet to the parent product.

syntax.python:
# Uses no optional arguments.
returnValue = selectionSets_var.add(entities)

# Uses optional arguments.
returnValue = selectionSets_var.add(entities, name)

add.return_description: Returns the created selection set or null in the case the selection set couldn't be created. This method can fail in the case where no entities are provided or if any of the provided entities are not selectable.

parameters.name: entities
parameters.description: An array of entities that will be in the created selection set. All entities must be in the context of the root component. This means if the entity isn't directly owned by the root component, it must be a proxy.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: The name of the selection set. This is an optional argument is if not specified, or an empty string is provided, Fusion will create a name for the selection set. If provided, the name should be unique with respect to other selection sets in the product. If a name is provided that is the same as an existing selection set, Fusion will append a counter to the name to make the name unique.   This is an optional argument whose default value is "".

classType.title: SelectionSets.classType Method
classType.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().
classType.return_description: Returns a string indicating the type of the object.

item.title: SelectionSets.item Method
item.description: Returns the specified SelectionSet object using an index into the collection.
item.return_description: Returns the specified SelectionSet or null if an invalid index was specified.

parameters.name: index
parameters.description: The index of the SelectionSet within the collection to return. The first item in the collection has an index of 0.

itemByName.title: SelectionSets.itemByName Method
itemByName.description: Returns the specified SelectionSet object using the name of the selection set.
itemByName.return_description: Returns the specified SelectionSet object or null if no SelectionSet object exists with the specified name.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: The name of the SelectionSet object to return.

count.title: SelectionSets.count Property
count.description: Returns the number of SelectionSet objects in the collection.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = selectionSets_var.count

isValid.title: SelectionSets.isValid Property
isValid.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = selectionSets_var.isValid

objectType.title: SelectionSets.objectType Property
objectType.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object. It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = selectionSets_var.objectType
